title: Friends and Feelings
description: Express your feelings and make friends everywhere!

scenes.speech: People who say their feelings are loved worldwide! 💖
scenes.title: Feelings in English
scenes.content: This lesson is about friends and feelings. When you can put feelings into words, you can connect across cultures and countries.

scenes.title: Happy Words
scenes.content: I'm happy. I'm so glad! Yay! Use different intensities and your emotion lands better.
scenes.highlight: I'm happy. / I'm so glad! / Yay!

scenes.title: Sad Words
scenes.content: I'm sad. I feel down. I miss you. Naming sadness invites care from people around you. Sharing beats holding it in.
scenes.highlight: I'm sad. / I feel down. / I miss you.

scenes.title: Angry Words
scenes.content: I'm angry. I'm upset. That's not fair. Don't shout — convey it as 'sad/troubled', and conversations stay open.
scenes.highlight: I'm angry. / I'm upset. / That's not fair.

scenes.title: Excited Words
scenes.content: I'm excited! I can't wait! Tests, trips, birthdays — putting excitement in words lights up the room.

scenes.speech: It's OK to say you're scared too!
scenes.title: Scared Words
scenes.content: I'm scared. I'm worried. I'm nervous. Honesty is not weakness — people trust those who can say it.
scenes.highlight: I'm scared. / I'm worried. / I'm nervous.

scenes.title: Words for Friends
scenes.content: You're my best friend. I'm here for you. In tough or joyful moments, these lines deepen any friendship.
scenes.highlight:
You're my best friend.
I'm here for you.

scenes.speech: 'Thank you' is the king of feelings!
scenes.title: Words of Gratitude
scenes.content: Thank you. I appreciate it. You made my day. Variety in gratitude makes hearts hear it deeper.
scenes.highlight:
Thank you.
I appreciate it.
You made my day.

scenes.speech: Pair them with phrases for asking too!
scenes.title: Asking About Feelings
scenes.content: How do you feel? Are you OK? People who can ask feelings are trusted twice as much as those who only speak.
scenes.highlight:
How do you feel?
Are you OK?

scenes.speech: Quiz! How do you say 'I'm so excited!' in English?
scenes.quizQuestion: Which is the natural English for 'I can't wait'?
scenes.quizExplanation: I'm excited! is the natural way to say it. 'I can't wait!' works too.
